security.title: Use of Hard-coded Password
security.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

evidence.explanation: LegacyAuditFilterTest contains hard-coded password strings passed directly to PlantSecurityProperties at lines 43 and 58: `new PlantSecurityProperties("configured-key")`. Although located in test code, these are literal hard-coded credential values embedded in source that should use a test-property placeholder or randomly generated value instead of a fixed string.

security.title: Insufficient Logging
security.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.

evidence.explanation: LegacyAuditFilter.doFilter() (line 37) logs only method and URI at INFO level and never logs security-critical outcomes such as missing or invalid API keys, unauthorized access attempts, or rejected requests — every request is always passed through regardless of credentials. GlobalExceptionHandler handles validation exceptions (MethodArgumentNotValidException) without any security-level logging of the violation. No component in the application logs authentication failures or authorization denials.

security.title: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
security.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.

evidence.explanation: LegacyAuditFilterTest hard-codes the credential string "configured-key" directly in source at lines 43 and 58 via `new PlantSecurityProperties("configured-key")`. Additionally, infra/resources.bicep sets the Key Vault secret value to the literal string 'REPLACE_ME_POST_DEPLOY' (line ~128), a placeholder credential committed to source control that could be deployed as-is to a live environment.
